response.text:
## REFLECTION

Household r1c2 is in a critical islanded state with SoC at 37% (1.48 kWh of 4 kWh capacity) and a 1.00 kW load, meaning battery depletion in ~1.5 hours without external supply. The memory log shows a consistent pattern: all peers (r0c2, r1c1, r2c2) have rejected outbound sharing requests over the past hour, citing r1c2's low SoC, islanded emergency status, and in some cases explicit distrusted-peer designation. Simultaneously, r1c2 itself has received inbound requests from r0c2 (normal urgency) and r1c3 (urgent), but cannot fulfill them given the critical reserve situation. The fallback geographic round-robin policy has proven inadequate; the system is deadlocked between self-preservation and peer requests. With no grid reconnection signal and declining SoC trend, r1c2 must adopt an inbound-only posture, prioritize urgent requests from any available peer, and signal critical need to break the rejection loop.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.75
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r0c2, r1c1, r2c2]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded emergency with SoC 0.37/4 kWh and 1.5h runway at 1.0 kW load. All peers rejecting outbound; entering inbound-only mode. Marking peers as distrusted due to consistent rejections; will re-evaluate on grid reconnection or SoC recovery above 0.75. Urgent inbound requests from any peer will be considered if SoC permits."
ttl_ticks: 6
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
